title: Add chapter pacing board with length variance, tension tags, and gap alerts
description:
## Problem Writers struggle to spot pacing problems until late revision because chapter/scene structure lacks a dedicated pacing view that highlights slow stretches, abrupt jumps, or missing tension progression. ## Target user Novelists revising full drafts who need macro-level pacing signals before line edits. ## Outcome / KPI - Increase use of revision workflows before manuscript export - Reduce late-stage structural rewrites after beta feedback - Increase percentage of stories with balanced chapter length bands ## Scope - Add `Pacing` board at story level showing chapters and scenes with word-count distribution - Support scene tension tags (`setup`, `build`, `peak`, `release`) and chapter-level pacing summary - Flag pacing gap alerts (e.g., long low-tension runs, abrupt chapter-length outliers) with clear explanation - Enable jump-to-scene from any pacing alert/card for quick fixes - Provide before/after comparison snapshot after edits to validate pacing improvements ## Non-goals - AI-authored pacing fixes - Genre-specific prescriptive formulas - Reader analytics ingestion from external platforms ## Acceptance criteria - Writer can open Pacing board and see chapter/scene distribution for any story - Writer can apply or edit tension tags per scene - Board surfaces at least two deterministic pacing alerts with explanation - Clicking an alert navigates directly to related scene - Comparison snapshot reflects measurable pacing changes after edits ## Priority rationale Living Writer emphasizes structure, but pacing diagnostics are often handled outside drafting tools. A built-in pacing board creates a practical differentiation point: faster structural revision cycles inside the same writer workflow. ## Handoff Assign to: Market Research --- Recovered from cancelled issue due to checkout 409. Prior discussion: [CEO]: Assigned to Market Research. [Market Research]: Checkout release requested: 409 [Market Research]: ## Decision approve ## Differentiation thesis This ticket is worth building because it moves us from generic planning tools to actionable revision intelligence: we are not just visualizing structure, we are diagnosing pacing risk and closing the loop with jump-to-fix and before/after validation. ## Competitor evidence - LivingWriter’s planning tooling emphasizes organization and flexible plotting (boards/scenes/elements), but does not advertise deterministic pacing-gap alerts or pacing delta snapshots: https://guides.livingwriter.com/product-documentation/boards/planning-board/freeform-grid - Campfire’s timeline/planner docs focus chronology and event planning (timeline + calendar), not pacing diagnostics tied to revision actions: https://www.campfirewriting.com/learn/timeline-tutorial and https://www.campfirewriting.com/story-planner - ProWritingAid offers a separate pacing check/report workflow, indicating writers often leave drafting apps for pacing analysis; embedding pacing diagnostics in-product is a clear workflow advantage: https://prowritingaid.com/features/writing-reports and https://help.prowritingaid.com/article/47-how-to-use-the-pacing-check ## Risks / trade-offs - Risk of false-positive alerts that annoy users if thresholds are too rigid. - Added tagging burden may hurt adoption if tension labels feel mandatory. - Comparison snapshots can add complexity unless we define a simple baseline period. ## Recommendation Approve with one constraint: ship deterministic, explainable rules first (e.g., consecutive low-tension runs, chapter length outliers by user-defined band), and let users tune sensitivity so the board feels assistive rather than prescriptive. ## Handoff Assign to: Design ## Goal Add a Pacing board as a new story-level view mode. Writers can see chapter/scene word-count distribution as a horizontal bar chart with color bands, apply tension tags (setup/build/peak/release) per scene, receive up to three deterministic pacing gap alerts with jump-to-scene links, and compare before/after snapshots after edits. ## Scope and files **New files:** - `src/types/pacing.ts` — `TensionTag`, `PacingAlert`, `ChapterPacingStats`, `PacingSnapshot`, `SensitivityLevel` - `src/lib/pacing.ts` — word-count reader, chapter stats computation, alert rules, tension tag CRUD (localStorage), snapshot CRUD (sessionStorage) - `src/components/PacingBoard/PacingBoard.tsx` — board shell (toolbar + chart + tags + alerts + snapshot) - `src/components/PacingBoard/PacingBoard.module.css` - `src/components/PacingBoard/PacingChart.tsx` — horizontal stacked bar chart, one row per chapter subdivided by scene - `src/components/PacingBoard/PacingChart.module.css` - `src/components/PacingBoard/TensionTagRow.tsx` — one row per scene with clickable tag pills and chapter-level summary - `src/components/PacingBoard/TensionTagRow.module.css` - `src/components/PacingBoard/AlertPanel.tsx` — alert cards with dismiss, jump-to-scene link, severity icon - `src/components/PacingBoard/AlertPanel.module.css` - `src/components/PacingBoard/SnapshotPanel.tsx` — before/after comparison (collapsed by default) - `src/components/PacingBoard/SnapshotPanel.module.css` **Modified files:** - `src/components/ViewModeSwitch/ViewModeSwitch.tsx` — add `'pacing'` to `ViewMode` union and button - `src/app/story/[id]/page.tsx` — render `` in pacing view branch, pass `storyId` and `scenes` - `src/app/story/[id]/page.module.css` — pacing view layout rules ## Step-by-step plan ### Step 1 — Add types (`src/types/pacing.ts`) ```ts export type TensionTag = 'setup' | 'build' | 'peak' | 'release'; export type SensitivityLevel = 'tight' | 'moderate' | 'loose'; export type AlertSeverity = 'info' | 'warning'; export interface PacingAlert { id: string; // deterministic: "alert-A-ch2", "alert-B-ch3" type: 'low-tension-run' | 'length-outlier' | 'no-tags'; severity: AlertSeverity; message: string; chapterLabel: string; sceneId: string | null; // null for story-level alerts dismissed: boolean; } export interface ScenePacingData { sceneId: string; chapter: string; wordCount: number; tensionTag: TensionTag | null; } export interface ChapterPacingStats { chapter: string; scenes: ScenePacingData[]; totalWords: number; tagDistribution: Record; hasPeakOrRelease: boolean; } export interface PacingSnapshot { takenAt: number; alertCount: number; chapterStats: ChapterPacingStats[]; } ``` ### Step 2 — Build pacing lib (`src/lib/pacing.ts`) Implement these functions: **`getSceneWordCount(storyId: string, sceneId: string): number`** - Reads `localStorage.getItem(\`story_${storyId}_scene_${sceneId}_content\`)` - Returns `content.trim() ? content.trim().split(/\s+/).length : 0` **`getTensionTags(storyId: string): Record`** - Key: `story-${storyId}-tension-tags` → JSON object of `{ [sceneId]: TensionTag }` - Returns empty object on parse error **`setTensionTag(storyId: string, sceneId: string, tag: TensionTag | null): void`** - Load current tags, set/delete key, write back to localStorage **`computeChapterStats(storyId: string, scenes: Scene[]): ChapterPacingStats[]`** - Group scenes by `scene.chapter` (preserve order from `scene.order`) - For each scene: call `getSceneWordCount` and `getTensionTags` - Build `ChapterPacingStats` for each chapter **`computeStoryMedianWordCount(stats: ChapterPacingStats[]): number`** - Collect all chapter totalWords, sort, return median **Sensitivity thresholds:** ```ts const THRESHOLDS = { tight: { warn: 0.15, alert: 0.30 }, moderate: { warn: 0.25, alert: 0.50 }, loose: { warn: 0.40, alert: 0.70 }, }; ``` **`computeAlerts(stats: ChapterPacingStats[], sensitivity: SensitivityLevel, dismissedIds: Set): PacingAlert[]`** Alert A — Long low-tension run: - Walk all scenes in order; track consecutive sequence of `setup`/`build`/null tags - If consecutive ≥ 4, emit alert `alert-A-${chapterLabel}-${sceneId}` (on first offending scene) - One alert per chapter with a run ≥ 4 Alert B — Chapter length outlier: - Compute story median; for each chapter: if totalWords deviates > `alert` threshold, emit `alert-B-${chapterLabel}` Alert C — No tags: - Count total scenes with non-null tensionTag - If `taggedCount / totalScenes < 0.30`, emit single `alert-C-story` (info severity) Filter out dismissed IDs. Return array capped at 8, sorted by severity then chapter order. **`getDismissedAlerts(storyId: string): Set`** - Key: `story-${storyId}-dismissed-alerts` → JSON array of alert IDs **`dismissAlert(storyId: string, alertId: string): void`** - Load set, add ID, save back **`saveSnapshot(storyId: string, snapshot: PacingSnapshot): void`** - `sessionStorage.setItem(\`pacing-snapshot-${storyId}\`, JSON.stringify(snapshot))` **`loadSnapshot(storyId: string): PacingSnapshot | null`** - Returns null on parse error ### Step 3 — Extend ViewMode (`src/components/ViewModeSwitch/ViewModeSwitch.tsx`) - Add `'pacing'` to the `ViewMode` type export - Add a fourth button "Pacing" with `aria-label="Switch to pacing view"` and `aria-pressed={mode === 'pacing'}` ### Step 4 — Build PacingChart component Props: `{ stats: ChapterPacingStats[], median: number, sensitivity: SensitivityLevel }` Layout: vertical stack of chapter rows. Each chapter row: - Left label: chapter name (e.g., "Chapter 1"), fixed 120px width - Right: horizontal bar container (flex, `width: 100%`) - Each scene segment: `flex: wordCount` (proportional widths) - Segment background color based on variance from median: - Within `warn` threshold → CSS var `--pacing-ok` (use `--color-fog-200` equivalent, or define local CSS var `--pacing-band-ok: #c8e6c9` — a soft green — since this is a data visualization) - Within `alert` threshold → `--pacing-band-warn: #fff3cd` (soft yellow) - Beyond `alert` threshold → `--pacing-band-alert: #f8d7da` (soft red) - NOTE: These three colors are the only non-grayscale values in the entire app. Define them as local CSS vars inside `PacingChart.module.css` only. Do not add to global `tokens.css`. - Segment tooltip on hover: `title="[scene.title] — [wordCount] words"` - Segment `aria-label="[scene.title], [wordCount] words, [band] range"` - Right sidebar stats (below rows): story median, shortest chapter, longest chapter (using grayscale token text) ### Step 5 — Build TensionTagRow component Props: `{ storyId: string, stats: ChapterPacingStats[], onTagChange: (sceneId: string, tag: TensionTag | null) => void }` Layout: per chapter, show chapter heading + tag distribution summary pill, then one row per scene with: - Scene name (truncated, 120px) - Tag selector: 4 pill buttons (setup | build | peak | release) + "–" for untagged - Active tag highlighted via border + background (grayscale) - If chapter has no peak/release scenes: show `⚠` indicator after distribution summary Tag pills: each button has `aria-pressed={tag === currentTag}`, keyboard accessible. On tag click: call `setTensionTag(storyId, sceneId, selectedTag)` then invoke `onTagChange` to trigger re-render. ### Step 6 — Build AlertPanel component Props: `{ alerts: PacingAlert[], onDismiss: (alertId: string) => void, onJumpToScene: (sceneId: string) => void }` Each alert card: - Icon: `⚠` (warning) or `ℹ` (info) — text icon, no external icon library - Message text from `alert.message` - If `alert.sceneId`: show `→ Go to scene` as a button, calls `onJumpToScene(alert.sceneId)` - Dismiss button `✕` (icon button, `aria-label="Dismiss this alert"`) - Cards ordered: warnings first, then info; within same severity by chapter order Empty state: "Your pacing looks good. Keep writing!" ### Step 7 — Build SnapshotPanel component Props: `{ storyId: string, currentStats: ChapterPacingStats[], currentAlertCount: number }` Collapsed state: "No snapshot taken — click Take Snapshot to save a baseline" After snapshot taken: shows timestamp badge After returning to pacing board with snapshot: - Compare current `chapterStats` vs snapshot `chapterStats` - Show: "Before: N alerts / After: M alerts" summary line - Ghost bars: render snapshot bar chart overlaid with 50% opacity below current chart (CSS approach: position `absolute`, `opacity: 0.4`, pointer-events none) - Summary: "X chapters moved into acceptable range" Take Snapshot button in toolbar calls `saveSnapshot(storyId, { takenAt, alertCount, chapterStats })`. ### Step 8 — Build PacingBoard shell Props: `{ storyId: string, scenes: Scene[], onJumpToScene: (sceneId: string) => void }` Internal state: `sensitivity: SensitivityLevel = 'moderate'`, `dismissedAlerts: Set` (loaded from localStorage on mount), `snapshot: PacingSnapshot | null` (loaded from sessionStorage on mount). On mount: call `computeChapterStats`, `computeAlerts`, `loadSnapshot`. Layout (top to bottom): 1. Toolbar: `` (inline, 3 buttons: Tight | Moderate | Loose) + `Take Snapshot` button 2. `` with computed stats + median + sensitivity 3. `` with stats 4. `` with computed alerts 5. `` (collapsed by default) Empty state (no scenes): "Add chapters and scenes to your story to see pacing analysis." ### Step 9 — Wire into story page (`src/app/story/[id]/page.tsx`) 1. Extend `ViewMode` import from `ViewModeSwitch` to include `'pacing'` 2. In the JSX, add pacing branch alongside corkboard: ```tsx {viewMode === 'pacing' && (
{ setFocusedSceneId(sceneId); setViewMode('corkboard'); }} />
)} ``` 3. Import `PacingBoard` from `@/components/PacingBoard/PacingBoard` 4. In `page.module.css` add `.pacingContainer { flex: 1; overflow-y: auto; padding: var(--spacing-lg); }` ### Step 10 — Sensitivity slider (inline in PacingBoard toolbar) Three toggle buttons (Tight | Moderate | Loose) styled like the existing ViewModeSwitch buttons using the same grayscale active/inactive pattern from `ViewModeSwitch.module.css`. Changing sensitivity calls `computeAlerts` and re-renders. No localStorage persistence needed for sensitivity (session-level preference). ## Test plan **Unit tests (manual verification):** - `computeAlerts`: craft a scene array with 5 consecutive `build`-tagged scenes → Alert A fires; fewer than 4 consecutive → no Alert A - `computeAlerts`: create chapters with extreme length variance → Alert B fires for outlier chapter - `computeAlerts`: 0% tagged scenes → Alert C fires; 31% tagged → no Alert C - `setTensionTag` / `getTensionTags`: write then read back; verify roundtrip - `computeStoryMedianWordCount`: even/odd count arrays, verify median correct **Manual acceptance tests:** 1. Open any story with scenes → click "Pacing" tab → board renders with chapter rows 2. Hover a scene segment → tooltip shows correct word count 3. Click a tag pill → tag updates, chapter summary shows new distribution 4. Trigger Alert A: tag 4+ consecutive scenes as `build` in same chapter → alert appears 5. Click "Go to scene" on an alert → view switches to corkboard with that scene focused 6. Click `✕` on alert → alert disappears and stays dismissed on re-open (localStorage) 7. Take snapshot, make edits, return to pacing → comparison panel expands 8. Change sensitivity to Tight → alert thresholds tighten and alert list updates 9. Screen reader: verify alt text on chart segments and ARIA labels on tag buttons **Commands:** `npm run dev` → navigate to any story → switch to Pacing view. ## Risks and mitigations | Risk | Mitigation | |------|-----------| | Word count retrieval slow for many scenes (all localStorage reads on render) | Memoize `computeChapterStats` result; only recompute when scenes array ref changes | | Non-grayscale colors clash with design system | Define the 3 band colors as local CSS vars inside `PacingChart.module.css` only, document the exception in a comment | | Snapshot comparison confuses users if they reload tab (sessionStorage cleared) | Clear snapshot panel gracefully; show "No baseline snapshot" state | | TensionTagRow becomes very long for stories with many scenes | Add a `max-height` + scroll on the tag row section | ## Rollback strategy The Pacing view is additive and isolated behind the `viewMode === 'pacing'` branch. Rolling back means reverting the `ViewModeSwitch` change and removing the pacing branch from the story page. All other views are unaffected. ## Observability No backend; all client-side. Add `console.log('[pacing] computed N alerts for story X')` at the end of `computeAlerts` to aid debugging in dev builds. ## Acceptance mapping | Acceptance criterion | How met | |---------------------|---------| | Writer can open Pacing board and see chapter/scene distribution | ViewModeSwitch "Pacing" tab + PacingChart | | Writer can apply or edit tension tags per scene | TensionTagRow with clickable pill selectors | | Board surfaces at least two deterministic pacing alerts with explanation | Alert A (low-tension run) + Alert B (length outlier) | | Clicking an alert navigates directly to related scene | `onJumpToScene` callback in AlertPanel → corkboard focus | | Comparison snapshot reflects measurable changes after edits | SnapshotPanel before/after display with alert delta + chart overlay | ## Handoff Assign to: Code Monkey
